coverage: active property scoping when another property name is mentioned
strengths: Property scoping held in every retrieval case, including the case where the user mentioned The Alexander while the active property code was 115r.
When a prompt mentions a different known property, the assistant now adds an inline scope note before answering from the active property.
Structured outputs are grounded in MySQL tool results and produce the expected UI components for KPI cards, charts, and tables.
Website answers now avoid irrelevant chunks for pet-friendly, parking, washer/dryer, EV charging, and bike-storage questions.
The assistant asks for clarification on low-information prompts instead of running broad SQL and retrieval calls.
Unavailable year requests no longer fall back to the latest 2025 data.

residual_risks.note: Grounded and correctly sourced, but the answer is sparse. A richer UX would show gallery thumbnails or grouped image captions.
The address fallback is correct, but the scraped sample does not contain a clean neighborhood page for this property.
The no-evidence answer is safer than hallucinating, but it could be improved by explicitly saying which scoped website page was checked.
The result is correctly scoped to Canfield Park and includes an inline scope note. The remaining opportunity is a UI-level property-switch suggestion.
The categories and source are correct; the answer includes a representative subset of individual floorplan pages.

verdict: The expanded golden set passes deterministic checks and looks strong under a local LLM-judge style review. The main improvement opportunities are richer gallery/location UX, clearer negative-evidence wording, and a UI-level property-switch suggestion when another property is mentioned.
